title: Savory Tender Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A pungent and tender one-pot dish featuring lamb, white chocolate, and yuzu-cure, made in a traditional Saudi Arabian style with low-calorie friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the yuzu juice and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the white chocolate chips until they are melted and the sauce is smooth.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Let the tagine cool completely before refrigerating or freezing it for later use.
